# Covex

> Covex is a non-custodial Kaspa covenant explorer and builder at https://hightable.pro.
> It indexes covenants on the Kaspa DAG, lets anyone build and deploy real on-chain
> covenants without writing script, and gives each covenant an interactive website UI.
> Covex never takes custody of funds: it indexes, builds scripts, and (for some kinds)
> attests outcomes, but the chain or the user's own keys move the money.

This file helps an AI agent understand and use Covex to help a user build covenants and
their interactive website UIs. Every endpoint below is real. Do not invent endpoints.

## What Covex is

- A read API and explorer over Kaspa covenants (mainnet, testnet-10, testnet-12).
- A no-code builder: pick a covenant kind, set its logic, deploy a real P2SH covenant.
- A website builder: each covenant gets a custom, interactive page (Puck-based).
- Honest by design: every covenant is labeled by HOW its outcome is actually enforced.

## Read API

Base URL: https://hightable.pro/api
All list endpoints are paginated with `limit` (max 200) and `offset`. Responses are JSON
and are not cached (live data). The same API powers the explorer UI.

- GET /covenants
  List covenants. Query: network (mainnet | testnet-10 | testnet-12), limit, offset,
  q (keyword, pipe = OR e.g. `chess|fide`), category, creator.
- GET /covenants/:id
  Full covenant detail including script_hex, enforcement reality, and custom UI config.
- GET /covenants/:id/actions
  Per-covenant activity history (deploy, tier upgrades, oracle resolutions, game updates).
- GET /address/:addr
  Public portfolio for a Kaspa address: covenants created, payments, totals.
- GET /resolve/:query?network=mainnet|testnet-10|testnet-12  (default mainnet)
  Smart-resolve a free-form query. Powers the explorer search bar and AI/API callers.
  Classifies the query and resolves KNS .kas domains to their owner address.
  Returns: { query, type ("kns"|"address"|"txid"|"covenant"|"keyword"), resolved (bool),
  address (string|null), covenant_id (string|null), network, network_hint
  ("mainnet"|"testnet"|null), kns ({name, owner}|null), note (string|null) }.
  KNS is indexed on mainnet and testnet-10 only; on testnet-12 resolved is false.
  KNS lookups are fail-closed and time-bounded: a lookup error never fabricates an owner.
- GET /events
  Recent activity feed across covenants. Query: network, limit.
- GET /stats
  Public platform statistics (covenants, TVL, tiers, categories, activity timeline).
  Query: network (all | mainnet | testnet-10 | testnet-12).
- GET /analytics
  Creator or global analytics. Query: creator.
- GET /covenant/catalog
  The catalog of deployable covenant kinds, each with its honest enforcement reality,
  builder, params, and a summary. Read this before suggesting a covenant kind.
- GET /openapi.json
  Machine-readable OpenAPI 3.0 schema for the read API.

## Covenant kinds and their honest enforcement reality

Covex labels every covenant by how its outcome is ACTUALLY enforced, strongest first.
Never describe a covenant as more enforced than its label. Source of truth: /covenant/catalog.

- on-chain: Kaspa consensus enforces the spend condition (a P2SH script). No oracle, no
  trust. Kinds: single-key P2SH, hashlock, absolute timelock (CLTV), N-of-M multisig,
  HTLC, 2-of-2 state-channel pot, dead-man's switch, relative timelock (CSV), and
  time-decaying multisig. Also the binary outcome selector unit (every branch is gated by
  a specific key, no Covex key in the redeem).
- hybrid: a real on-chain script gates release, but an oracle supplies a signature the
  script checks (e.g. an oracle co-signature verified on-chain). Trust the signer set, not
  pure math. Kinds: oracle-enforced (2-of-2 of oracle + winner), oracle escrow (2-player
  pot), and their timeout-refundable variants (a CSV refund branch if the oracle goes
  silent). The oracle co-signs only a verified outcome.
- oracle-attested: the outcome is asserted by the Covex oracle's real BIP340 signature,
  but funds are NOT script-gated to that signature; they move by an ordinary transaction.
  Trust the oracle. Kind: oracle-resolved game / market (off-chain). Prefer an on-chain or
  hybrid kind when value is at stake.
- decorative: no enforcement. A metadata / marker covenant; the chain enforces nothing
  about the outcome. Not for value at stake.

Honesty rules for an agent using Covex:
- Never overclaim enforcement. Use the catalog's reality label verbatim.
- For hybrid and oracle-attested kinds, state the oracle-liveness caveat: if the oracle is
  unavailable, an outcome may not be attested; the refundable variants add a timeout so the
  funder can reclaim the stake.
- ZK circuits on Covex are oracle-verified off-chain, not on-chain (with narrow exceptions).
  Do not describe them as on-chain ZK verification.

## Covenant lifecycle and the build flow

A covenant on Covex moves through: built -> deployed (funds locked to the P2SH) ->
discovered/indexed by the crawler -> (optionally) resolved or claimed.

The 5-step build flow:
1. Pick: choose a covenant kind from the catalog.
2. Logic: set its parameters (keys, hashes, timelocks, stake, circuit type).
3. Deploy: lock real funds to the generated P2SH covenant on the chosen network.
4. Design: build the covenant's interactive website UI (Puck-based, below).
5. Share: publish the covenant page; anyone can view, interact, and verify it.

## The interactive website UI

Each covenant gets a custom page on Covex. The UI is built with a Puck-based editor and
stored as a custom_ui config on the covenant. It renders interactive blocks (text, media,
allowlisted video, enforcement badges, action panels) on the covenant page, so a covenant
can present like a small website while the explorer still shows the honest enforcement
reality and the raw script.

## The non-custodial claim path

Covex does not custody funds, so claiming is non-custodial:
- For script-enforced kinds and revealed-secret branches, a client-side redeemer builds and
  signs the spend in the browser; the private key never leaves the client.
- An offline cold-recovery tool (a standalone, self-contained HTML page built from the repo
  at frontend/tools/cold-recovery/) lets a winner or refund-key holder claim from a
  file:// page with no Covex frontend and no Covex backend in the loop. Its signing bytes
  are CI-proven byte-for-byte identical to the backend satisfier.
- Honest matrix: script-enforced and revealed-secret branches are offline-claimable; oracle
  (hybrid / oracle-attested) kinds depend on the oracle for the winning outcome and offer a
  timeout refund rather than unconditional offline claim.

## Summary for an agent

To help a user build on Covex: read /covenant/catalog for the available kinds and their
honest enforcement reality, pick the kind that matches the user's trust requirement (prefer
on-chain), use the read API to inspect existing covenants and addresses, use /resolve to
turn a .kas name or pasted string into an address or covenant, and always communicate the
enforcement reality and oracle-liveness caveats truthfully.
